body: Hey Daichi, just checking in on the Greenlink dashboard project. Where are we with the API integration? The stakeholders are asking for a status update before Thursday's review. Let me know what you've got so far and if there are any blockers I should be aware of.
snippet: Hey Daichi, just checking in on the Greenlink dashboard project. Where are we wi...
subject: Project update

body: Hi Kevin, things are moving along well. The API integration is about 80% complete — I've got the auth flow and data endpoints working, and I'm currently debugging a rate-limiting issue on the analytics feed. I should have a full status doc ready by Wednesday morning. Let me know if you need anything specific for Thursday's review and I'll make sure it's covered.
snippet: Hi Kevin, things are moving along well. The API integration is about 80% complet...
subject: Re: Project update

body: Hi Daichi, I'm putting together the agenda for Thursday's team meeting. So far I have: sprint retrospective, Q3 roadmap review, and a quick update on new hire onboarding. Are there any items you'd like me to add? Please send them over by end of day Tuesday so I can finalize the doc.
snippet: Hi Daichi, I'm putting together the agenda for Thursday's team meeting. So far I...
subject: Team meeting Thursday

body: Hey Kevin, thanks for looping me in. I'd like to add a brief discussion about the Greenlink API performance issues we've been seeing — I think the team should be aware and it might affect sprint planning. Also, could we carve out five minutes to discuss the staging environment setup? A few of us have been blocked on that. I'll keep both items short.
snippet: Hey Kevin, thanks for looping me in. I'd like to add a brief discussion about th...
subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday

body: Good additions — I'll slot the API performance discussion right after the retrospective and the staging environment item at the end. I've updated the agenda doc and shared it in the team channel. See you Thursday at 10!
snippet: Good additions — I'll slot the API performance discussion right after the retros...
subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday

body: Hey, I know you've got your 1:1 with Marcus next week. Have you had a chance to put together your talking points yet? You mentioned last month that you wanted to bring up the promotion timeline — don't let that slip through the cracks again like it did in March.
snippet: Hey, I know you've got your 1:1 with Marcus next week. Have you had a chance to ...
subject: Manager 1:1 prep

body: Ha, you're right — I almost forgot. I started jotting down some notes last night. I'm planning to bring up the promotion conversation, the resource allocation for Q3, and maybe get his thoughts on me leading the new dashboard project. Still need to organize it into something coherent though. I'll work on it tonight.
snippet: Ha, you're right — I almost forgot. I started jotting down some notes last night...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body: Those sound like solid topics. For the promotion piece, make sure you come with concrete wins — your work on the Greenlink integration and that stakeholder presentation you pulled off in April. Don't go in empty-handed. Also think about asking what milestones HE needs to see from you. Makes the conversation more two-way and shows self-awareness.
snippet: Those sound like solid topics. For the promotion piece, make sure you come with ...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body: That's really good framing — I'll structure it that way. I'm going to draft a quick one-pager tonight and maybe run it by you before I go in. Thanks for pushing me on this, I genuinely would have shown up with nothing. Love you.
snippet: That's really good framing — I'll structure it that way. I'm going to draft a qu...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body: Hey, just a heads up — I noticed your data privacy compliance training expires at the end of this month. I know you said you'd do it 'this weekend' about three weekends ago now. Just making sure it's actually on your radar this time before it becomes an HR issue.

body: Hey, I've been thinking about what to do for your mom's birthday. She mentioned brunch a few weeks ago and I think that's a really sweet idea. What if we planned something for Sunday the 13th? The kids would love seeing her and it's way less chaotic than a dinner situation.

snippet: That's a great idea — she'd really love that. The 13th works for me. Should we b...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body: I was leaning toward hosting at home — it's more personal and she always says she prefers it anyway. I can handle the food if you sort the decorations and pick up flowers. We could do a little mimosa bar and keep it relaxed. I'll invite your sister too.
snippet: I was leaning toward hosting at home — it's more personal and she always says sh...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body: Hey, I got the renewal notice for our home security monitoring service today. They're bumping the price up about $12 a month from last year. Before I just auto-renew, have you had a chance to look at any alternatives? I feel like we should at least compare options first.
snippet: Hey, I got the renewal notice for our home security monitoring service today. Th...
subject: Renewal options

body: I haven't looked yet but I've been meaning to. I'll check out a few competitors tonight — I think Ring and SimpliSafe both have plans in a similar range. What's our current monthly cost? That'll help me compare apples to apples.
snippet: I haven't looked yet but I've been meaning to. I'll check out a few competitors ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: We're at $49.99 a month right now for the pro plan with professional monitoring. The renewal offer bumps it to $61.99. I've been happy with the service overall but that's a pretty significant jump. If you can find something comparable for less, I'm open to switching.
snippet: We're at $49.99 a month right now for the pro plan with professional monitoring....
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Okay, I spent some time looking. SimpliSafe's equivalent plan is about $29.99 a month and the reviews are solid. The main thing we'd give up is the outdoor camera integration, but honestly I don't think we've used that feature more than twice. Might be worth switching if we're okay with a short setup process.
snippet: Okay, I spent some time looking. SimpliSafe's equivalent plan is about $29.99 a ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: That's a huge savings over the year. Let's go with SimpliSafe — you're right that we barely touch the outdoor cameras. Can you handle the cancellation and new account setup? Just make sure there's no gap in coverage between the two. I don't want to be unmonitored for any stretch.
snippet: That's a huge savings over the year. Let's go with SimpliSafe — you're right tha...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Hey, I got an invoice from the landscaping company and something looks off. They billed us for four visits in May but I'm pretty sure they only came three times — I specifically remember them skipping the week of the 12th because of the rain. Do you have any record of what was scheduled? I don't want to just pay it without checking.
snippet: Hey, I got an invoice from the landscaping company and something looks off. They...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body: You're right — I remember the same thing. I actually have an email from them confirming the skip on May 12th due to weather. I'll forward it to you so you have documentation when you contact them. They've always been good about correcting billing errors, so it should be a pretty quick fix.
snippet: You're right — I remember the same thing. I actually have an email from them con...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body: Hey, I got the lease renewal paperwork from the management office. They're raising rent by 6%, which works out to about $145 more a month. The renewal deadline is July 31st. Do you want to sit down this weekend and talk through whether we stay, try to negotiate, or start looking at other options?
snippet: Hey, I got the lease renewal paperwork from the management office. They're raisi...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body: Yeah, let's definitely talk it through. A 6% increase feels steep given how long we've been here without any issues. I think it's worth trying to negotiate first — we're solid tenants with a clean payment history and they know it. If they won't budge, maybe we use this as the push to seriously look at buying. Saturday morning work?
snippet: Yeah, let's definitely talk it through. A 6% increase feels steep given how long...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body: Saturday morning works perfectly. I'll pull together our payment history and a few rental comps in the area so we have something concrete to reference if we negotiate. And yes — maybe this is finally the nudge we need to look at buying. The market has been calming down. Let's lay everything out Saturday and make a real decision.
snippet: Saturday morning works perfectly. I'll pull together our payment history and a f...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body: Hey! Sending over a couple of links from newsletters I read this week that I thought you'd find interesting. First is a piece from Stratechery on how vertical AI is eating horizontal platforms — feels pretty relevant to what your team is building right now. Second is a short essay on async work culture that honestly made me rethink a few things about how we structure our evenings.
snippet: Hey! Sending over a couple of links from newsletters I read this week that I tho...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: Oh nice, I've been meaning to catch up on Stratechery — I've missed a few weeks. I'll read both tonight. Did you finish the async piece? I'm curious what specifically made you rethink things. Are you hinting that I should stop checking Slack at 10 PM?
snippet: Oh nice, I've been meaning to catch up on Stratechery — I've missed a few weeks....
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: Not just hinting — stating it outright. But yes, I finished it. The main argument is that productive async culture requires real offline hours, not just 'no meetings.' The author makes a good point that checking messages late actually signals to your team that you're always reachable, which creates pressure for everyone. Felt very on the nose for us.
snippet: Not just hinting — stating it outright. But yes, I finished it. The main argumen...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: Okay, that landed. I read the async piece over lunch and you're right — I'm going to try a hard cutoff at 8 PM this week and see how it feels. Thanks for sharing these. This is exactly the kind of thing I'd never find on my own. Keep sending them.
